content.problemStatement:
There are NNN students standing in a canteen queue, numbered 111 to NNN from left to right. For each valid iii, the iii-th student has a height hih_ihi​.

Two students iii and jjj can see each other if there are no taller students standing between them. Formally, students iii and jjj (i<ji \lt ji<j) can see each other if for each integer kkk (i<k<ji \lt k \lt ji<k<j), hk≤hi,hjh_k \le h_i, h_jhk​≤hi​,hj​.

For each valid iii, determine the number cic_ici​ of students that have the same height as student iii and can be seen by student iii.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (30 points):** the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10310^3103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers h1,h2,…,hNh_1, h_2, \ldots, h_Nh1​,h2​,…,hN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers c1,c2,…,cNc_1, c_2, \ldots, c_Nc1​,c2​,…,cN​.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Student 333 can see students 222 and 444, but only student 222 has the same height (height 222). On the other hand, student 333 cannot see student 555 since student 444 is taller and therefore blocking the view.
